Ini laguku sepanjang masa tanpa akhir
Yesus menuntunku sepanjang jalan. Amin.

title_en: Teach Me Thy Way, O Lord

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: Thy Loving-kindness, Lord, Is Good and Free
title_ko: 여호와여, 주의 인자하심은 선하고 값이 없으시니이다 (자체번역)

lines.en: Thy lovingkindness, Lord, is good and free:
lines.ko: 주님, 당신의 사랑은 선하고 값없이 베푸십니다.

lines.en: In tender mercy turn thou unto me;
Hide not thy face from me in my distress,
lines.ko: 내가 고난 중에 있을 때 주의 얼굴을 나에게서 숨기지 마소서.

lines.en: In mercy hear my pray'r, thy servant bless.
lines.ko: 자비로이 내기도를 들어주십시오. 당신의 종이 축복합니다.

lines.en: Needy and sorrowful, to thee I cry;
Let thy salvation set my soul on high;
Then I will sing and praise thy holy Name,
lines.ko: 그리하면 내가 주의 거룩하신 이름을 노래하고 찬양하리이다

lines.en: My thankful song thy mercy shall proclaim.
lines.ko: 나의 감사의 노래는 당신의 자비가 선포될 것입니다.

lines.en: With joy the meek shall see my soul restored;
lines.ko: 온유한 자들은 내 영혼이 회복되는 것을 기쁨으로 볼 것입니다.

lines.en: Your heart shall live, ye saints that seek the Lord;
lines.ko: 주님을 찾는 성도들아, 너희 마음은 살아날 것이다.

lines.en: He helps the needy and regards their cries,
lines.ko: 그는 궁핍한 자를 도우시며 그들의 부르짖음을 돌아보시며

lines.en: Those in distress the Lord will not despise.
lines.ko: 여호와께서는 환난 중에 있는 자를 멸시하지 아니하시리로다

lines.en: Let heav'n above his grace and glory tell
lines.ko: 그의 은혜와 영광 위에 높이 올라가서 말하게 하소서

lines.en: Let earth and sea and all that in them dwell;
lines.ko: 땅과 바다와 그 가운데 모든 것이 거하게 하라.

lines.en: Salvation to his people God will give,
And they that love his Name with him shall live.
lines.ko: 그리고 그와 함께 그의 이름을 사랑하는 자는 살리라.

title_en: To God My Earnest Voice I Raise

lines.en: From Psalm 142
To God my earnest voice I raise,
To God my voice imploring prays;
Before his face my grief I show
lines.ko: 그의 얼굴 앞에 내 슬픔을 보여주네

lines.en: And tell my trouble and my woe.
lines.ko: 그리고 나의 고난과 불행을 말하여 주십시오.

lines.en: When gloom and sorrow compass me,
The path I take is known to thee,
And all the toils that foes do lay
To snare thy servant in his way.
lines.ko: 주의 종의 길을 올무에 빠뜨리려 함이니이다

lines.en: O Lord, my Saviour, now to thee,
lines.ko: 오 주님, 나의 구세주여, 이제 당신께

lines.en: Without a hope besides, I flee,
To thee, my shelter from the strife,
My portion in the land of life.
Be thou my help when troubles throng,
lines.ko: 환난이 닥쳤을 때 나의 도움이 되소서.

lines.en: For I am weak and foes are strong;
My captive soul from prison bring,
And thankful praises I will sing.

title_en: Why Should Cross and Trial Grieve Me?
title_ko: 왜 십자가와 시련이 나를 슬프게 해야 합니까? (자체번역)

lines.en: Why should cross and trial grieve me?
lines.ko: 왜 십자가와 시련이 나를 슬프게 해야 합니까?

lines.en: Christ is near
With his cheer;
Never will he leave me.
lines.ko: 그는 결코 나를 떠나지 않을 것입니다.

lines.en: Who can rob me of the heaven
lines.ko: 누가 나에게서 천국을 빼앗을 수 있겠는가?

lines.en: That God's Son
For my own
To my faith hath given?
God oft gives me days of gladness;
lines.ko: 하나님은 종종 나에게 기쁨의 날을 주십니다.

lines.en: Shall I grieve
If he give
Seasons, too, of sadness?
God is good and tempers ever
All my ill,
And he will
Wholly leave me never.
Death cannot destroy for ever;
From our fears,
Cares, and tears
It will us deliver.
It will close life's mournful story,
Make a way
That we may
Enter heav'nly glory.
Lord, my Shepherd, take me to thee.
lines.ko: 나의 목자이신 주님, 저를 당신께 데려가 주십시오.

lines.en: Thou art mine;
I was thine,
Even ere I knew thee.
I am thine, for thou hast bought me;
lines.ko: 나는 당신 것입니다. 당신이 나를 사셨기 때문입니다.

lines.en: Lost I stood,
But thy blood
Free salvation brought me.
Thou art mine; I love and own thee.
lines.ko: 너는 내 것이다. 나는 당신을 사랑하고 소유합니다.

lines.en: Light of Joy,
Ne'er shall I
From my heart dethrone thee.
Saviour, let me soon behold thee
lines.ko: 구원자여, 내가 곧 당신을 뵙게 하소서

lines.en: Face to face,
May thy grace
Evermore enfold me!

title_en: Take Up Your Cross, the Savior Said

lines.en: "Take up thy cross," the Saviour said,
"If thou wouldst my disciple be;
Take up thy cross with willing heart,
And humbly follow after me."
Take up thy cross; let not its weight
lines.ko: 네 십자가를 지라. 그 무게를 두지 마십시오

lines.en: Fill thy weak soul with vain alarm;
lines.ko: 당신의 연약한 영혼을 헛된 경각심으로 채우십시오.

lines.en: His strength shall bear thy spirit up,
lines.ko: 그분의 힘이 당신의 영혼을 지탱해 주실 것입니다.

lines.en: And brace thy heart and nerve thine arm.
lines.ko: 그리고 당신의 심장과 신경을 당신의 팔에 단단히 고정시키십시오.

lines.en: Take up thy cross; nor heed the shame,
lines.ko: 네 십자가를 지라. 부끄러움에 개의치도 말고,

lines.en: And let thy foolish pride be still;
lines.ko: 그리고 네 어리석은 교만을 잠잠하게 하라.

lines.en: Thy Lord refused not e'en to die
lines.ko: 당신의 주님은 죽는 것을 거부하지 않으셨습니다

lines.en: Upon a cross, on Calv'ry's hill.
Take up thy cross, then, in his strength,
lines.ko: 그러므로 그의 능력으로 네 십자가를 지고

lines.en: And calmly sin's wild deluge brave;
lines.ko: 그리고 침착하게 죄의 대홍수를 용감하게 대하십시오.

lines.en: 'Twill guide thee to a better home,
It points to glory o'er the grave.
Take up thy cross, and follow on,
Nor think till death to lay it down;
lines.ko: 죽을 때까지 내려놓을 생각도 하지 마십시오.

lines.en: For only he who bears the cross
May hope to wear the glorious crown.

title_en: I Want Jesus to Walk with Me
title_ko: 나는 예수님이 나와 함께 걷기를 원합니다 (자체번역)

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: O Lord, Be Gracious to Me

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: Give Thanks unto the Lord, Jehovah

lines.en: From Psalm 118:1–9, 17–25
Give thanks unto the Lord, Jehovah,
For he is good, O praise his Name!
Let Israel say: The Lord be praised,
His mercy ever is the same.
Let Aaron's house now praise Jehovah;
The Lord is good, O praise his Name;
Let all that fear the Lord extol him,
His mercy ever is the same.
In a large place the Lord hath set me,
In my distress he heard my cry;
lines.ko: 내가 환난 중에 내 부르짖음을 들으셨으니

lines.en: I will not fear; the Lord is with me,
lines.ko: 나는 두려워하지 않을 것이다. 주님이 나와 함께 계십니다.

lines.en: What can man do, when God is nigh?
lines.ko: 하나님이 가까이 계시면 사람이 무엇을 할 수 있겠는가?

lines.en: The Lord is chief among my helpers;
lines.ko: 여호와는 나를 돕는 자 중에 으뜸이시니

lines.en: And I shall see my foes o'erthrown:
lines.ko: 그리고 나는 내 적들이 무너지는 것을 보게 될 것입니다.

lines.en: Far better than in man or princes,
My trust I place in God alone.
I shall not die, but live, declaring
The works of God, who tried me sore,
And chastened me; but in his mercy
lines.ko: 그리고 나를 징계하셨습니다. 그러나 그의 자비하심으로

lines.en: Not unto death hath giv'n me o'er.
lines.ko: 죽음에 이르기까지 나에게 o'er를 주지 않았습니다.

lines.en: The gates of righteousness set open,
The gate of God! I'll enter in
To praise thee, Lord, who pray'r hast answered,
lines.ko: 주님을 찬양하기 위해 기도하신 분이 응답하셨습니다.

lines.en: And savedst me from all my sin.
The stone—O Lord, it is thy doing—
lines.ko: 그 돌은 - 오 주님, 그것은 당신이 만드신 것입니다 -

lines.en: The stone, the builders did despise,
Is made the headstone of the corner,
And it is marv'lous in our eyes.
This is the day, of days most glorious,
The Lord hath made; we'll joy and sing:
lines.ko: 주님께서 만드셨습니다. 우리는 기뻐하며 노래할 것입니다:

lines.en: Send now prosperity, we pray thee;
And, O our God, salvation bring!
lines.ko: 그리고 오 우리 하나님, 구원을 주소서!

title_en: Now Israel May Say, and That in Truth
title_ko: 이제 이스라엘이 말할 수 있으며 그것은 진실입니다 (자체번역)

lines.en: From Psalm 124
Now Israel may say, and that in truth,
lines.ko: 이제 이스라엘은 다음과 같이 말할 수 있습니다.

lines.en: If that the Lord had not our right maintained,
lines.ko: 만약 주님께서 우리의 권리를 지키지 않으셨다면,

lines.en: If that the Lord had not with us remained,
lines.ko: 만일 여호와께서 우리와 함께 계시지 아니하셨더라면

lines.en: When cruel men against us rose to strive,
lines.ko: 우리를 대적하는 잔인한 자들이 일어나 싸우려고 했을 때,

lines.en: We surely had been swallowed up alive.
lines.ko: 우리는 분명히 산 채로 삼켜졌을 것입니다.

lines.en: Yea when their wrath against us fiercely rose,
lines.ko: 그렇습니다. 우리를 향한 그들의 분노가 격렬해졌을 때,

lines.en: The swelling tide had o'er us spread its wave,
lines.ko: 부풀어오르는 조수는 우리에게 파도를 퍼뜨리게 했고,

lines.en: The raging stream had then become our grave,
lines.ko: 성난 시냇물은 그때 우리의 무덤이 되었고,

lines.en: The surging flood, in proudly swelling roll,
Most surely then had overwhelmed our soul.
lines.ko: 그렇다면 확실히 우리의 영혼을 압도했을 것입니다.

lines.en: Blest be the Lord who made us not their prey:
lines.ko: 우리를 먹이로 삼지 아니하신 여호와를 찬송할지어다

lines.en: As from the snare a bird escapeth free,
Their net us rent and so escaped are we:
Our only help is in Jehovah's Name,
lines.ko: 우리의 유일한 도움은 여호와의 이름에 있습니다.

lines.en: Who made the earth and all the heav'nly frame.
lines.ko: 땅과 하늘의 모든 구조를 만드신 분.

title_en: Come, Ye Disconsolate, Where’er Ye Languish
title_ko: 오십시오, 너희는 낙담하고, 너희가 괴로워하는 곳은 어디인가? (자체번역)

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: Leaning on the Everlasting Arms

lines.en: What a fellowship, what a joy divine,
lines.id: Betapa rendahnya perasaan, betapa menyenangkannya ilahi,

lines.en: Leaning on the everlasting arms;
lines.id: Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi

lines.en: What a blessedness, what a peace is mine,
lines.id: Betapa berkahnya, betapa damai sejahtera yang kumiliki,

lines.en: Leaning on the everlasting arms.
lines.id: Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi.

lines.ko: 주의 팔에 그 크신 팔에 안기세
lines.id: Bersandar, bersandar,
Aman dan terlindungi dari semua alarm,
Bersandar, bersandar,
Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi.

lines.en: Leaning, leaning,
lines.id: Oh, betapa manisnya berjalan di jalan peziarah ini,

lines.en: Safe and secure from all alarms;
lines.id: Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi

lines.en: Leaning, leaning,
lines.id: Oh, betapa terangnya jalan itu tumbuh dari hari ke hari,

lines.en: Leaning on the everlasting arms.
lines.id: Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi.

lines.ko: 주의 팔에 그 크신 팔에 안기세
lines.id: Bersandar, bersandar,
Aman dan terlindungi dari semua alarm,
Bersandar, bersandar,
Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi.

lines.en: Oh, how sweet to walk in this pilgrim way,
lines.id: Apa yang harus aku takuti, apa yang harus aku takuti,

lines.en: Leaning on the everlasting arms;
lines.id: Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi

lines.en: Oh, how bright the path grows from day to day,
lines.id: Aku telah diberkati kedamaian dengan Tuhanku begitu dekat,

lines.en: Leaning on the everlasting arms.
lines.id: Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi.

lines.ko: 주의 팔에 그 크신 팔에 안기세
lines.id: Bersandar, bersandar,
Aman dan terlindungi dari semua alarm,
Bersandar, bersandar,
Bersandar pada lengan yang abadi.

lines.en: What have I to dread, what have I to fear,
Leaning on the everlasting arms?
I have blessed peace with my Lord so near,
Leaning on the everlasting arms.

alt_titles: Leaning on the everlasting arms
What a fellowship, what a joy divine
title_id: Persahabatan yang luar biasa, Kegembiraan yang luar biasa

title_en: My Anchor Holds

lines.en: Though the angry surges roll
On my tempest-driven soul,
I am peaceful, for I know,
lines.ko: 나는 평화롭다. 왜냐하면 나는 알고 있기 때문이다.

lines.en: Wildly though the winds may blow,
I've an anchor safe and sure,
That can evermore endure.
And it holds, my anchor holds;
lines.ko: 그리고 그것은 버티고 있습니다. 나의 닻은 버티고 있습니다.

lines.en: Blow your wildest then, O gale,
lines.ko: 그렇다면 가장 거칠게 불어라, 오 강풍이여,

lines.en: On my bark so small and frail:
lines.ko: 너무 작고 연약한 내 껍질 위에:

lines.en: By his grace I shall not fail,
lines.ko: 그의 은혜로 나는 실패하지 않을 것이다.

lines.en: For my anchor holds, my anchor holds.
lines.ko: 내 닻이 붙잡기 위해서는 나의 닻이 붙잡혀 있습니다.

lines.en: Mighty tides about me sweep,
lines.ko: 나를 둘러싼 거대한 파도가 휩쓸고 가네

lines.en: Perils lurk within the deep,
Angry clouds o'ershade the sky,
And the tempest rises high;
Still I stand the tempest's shock,
lines.ko: 아직도 나는 폭풍우의 충격을 견디고 있습니다.

lines.en: For my anchor grips the Rock.
I can feel the anchor fast
As I meet each sudden blast,
And the cable, though unseen,
Bears the heavy strain between;
Through the storm I safely ride,
Till the turning of the tide.
Troubles almost 'whelm the soul;
Griefs like billows o'er me roll;
Tempters seek to lure astray;
Storms obscure the light of day:
But in Christ I can be bold,
lines.ko: 그러나 그리스도 안에서 나는 담대할 수 있습니다.

lines.en: I've an anchor that shall hold.

alt_titles: My anchor holds
Though the angry surges roll

title_en: His Eye Is on the Sparrow

lines.en: Why should I feel discouraged,
Why should the shadows come,
Why should my heart be lonely
And long for heav'n and home,
When Jesus is my portion?
My constant Friend is he:
His eye is on the sparrow,
And I know he watches me;
lines.ko: 그리고 나는 그가 나를 지켜보고 있다는 것을 압니다.

lines.en: His eye is on the sparrow,
And I know he watches me.
lines.ko: 그리고 나는 그가 나를 지켜보고 있다는 것을 압니다.

lines.en: "Let not your heart be troubled,"
His tender Word I hear,
And resting on his goodness,
I lose my doubt and fear;
Though by the path he leadeth
But one step I may see:
lines.ko: 그러나 한 단계를 보면 다음과 같습니다.

lines.en: Whenever I am tempted,
Whenever clouds arise,
When songs give place to sighing,
When hope within me dies,
I draw the closer to him,
From care he sets me free;

alt_titles: Why should I feel discouraged

title_en: A Shelter in the Time of Storm

lines.en: The Lord's our Rock, in him we hide,
lines.ko: 여호와는 우리의 반석이시니 우리가 그 안에 숨는도다

lines.en: A shelter in the time of storm;
Secure whatever ill betide,
A shelter in the time of storm.
Oh, Jesus is a Rock in a weary land,
A weary land, a weary land,
Oh, Jesus is a Rock in a weary land,
A shelter in the time of storm.
A shade by day, defense by night,
A shelter in the time of storm;
No fears alarm, no foes affright,
lines.ko: 두려움에 놀라는 일도 없고, 겁에 질린 적도 없고,

lines.en: A shelter in the time of storm.
The raging storms may round us beat,
lines.ko: 맹렬한 폭풍이 우리를 휩쓸 수도 있고,

lines.en: A shelter in the time of storm;
We'll never leave our safe retreat,
lines.ko: 우리는 안전한 피난처를 결코 떠나지 않을 것입니다.

lines.en: A shelter in the time of storm.
O Rock Divine, O Refuge dear,
A shelter in the time of storm;
Be thou our helper ever near,
A shelter in the time of storm.

alt_titles: A shelter in the time of storm
The Lord's our Rock, in him we hide

title_en: O Lord, I Love You, My Shield, My Tower
title_ko: 오 주님, 당신을 사랑합니다, 나의 방패, 나의 탑 (자체번역)

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: Sometimes a Light Surprises

lines.en: Sometimes a light surprises
The Christian while he sings;
It is the Lord, who rises
With healing in his wings:
When comforts are declining,
He grants the soul again
A season of clear shining,
To cheer it after rain.
In holy contemplation
We sweetly then pursue
The theme of God's salvation,
And find it ever new;
lines.ko: 그리고 그것이 항상 새로운 것임을 발견하십시오.

lines.en: Set free from present sorrow,
We cheerfully can say,
lines.ko: 우리는 유쾌하게 이렇게 말할 수 있습니다.

lines.en: Let the unknown tomorrow
Bring with it what it may.
It can bring with it nothing
But he will bear us through;
lines.ko: 그러나 그분은 우리를 견디어 주실 것입니다.

lines.en: Who gives the lilies clothing
Will clothe his people too:
Beneath the spreading heavens
No creature but is fed;
lines.ko: 먹이를 주는 것 외에는 어떤 생물도 없습니다.

lines.en: And he who feeds the ravens
Will give his children bread.
Though vine nor fig tree neither
Their wonted fruit shall bear,
Though all the field should wither,
Nor flocks nor herds be there;
Yet God the same abiding,
His praise shall tune my voice,
lines.ko: 그분을 찬양하면 내 목소리가 조화를 이룰 것입니다.

lines.en: For, while in him confiding,
lines.ko: 왜냐하면 그에게 확신을 갖고 있는 동안,

lines.en: I cannot but rejoice.

title_en: I Waited for the Lord Most High

lines.en: Psalm 40:1-5
I waited for the Lord most high,
And he inclined to hear my cry;
lines.ko: 그리고 그는 내 부르짖음을 듣고자 하셨습니다.

lines.en: He took me from destruction's pit
And from the miry clay;
Upon a rock he set my feet,
And steadfast made my way.
A new and joyful song of praise
He taught my thankful heart to raise;
lines.ko: 그는 내 감사하는 마음을 키우도록 가르쳤습니다.

lines.en: And many, seeing me restored,
lines.ko: 그리고 많은 사람들이 내가 회복된 것을 보고,

lines.en: Shall fear the Lord and trust;
And blest are they that trust the Lord,
The humble and the just.
O Lord my God, how manifold
lines.ko: 오 주 나의 하나님, 얼마나 다양합니까?

lines.en: Thy wondrous works which I behold,
And all thy loving, gracious thought
lines.ko: 그리고 당신의 모든 사랑스럽고 은혜로운 생각

lines.en: Thou hast bestowed on man;
To count thy mercies I have sought,
lines.ko: 내가 구한 당신의 자비를 헤아리기 위해,

lines.en: But boundless is their span.

title_en: Moment by Moment

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: Through All the Changing Scenes of Life

lines.en: From Psalm 34:1-4, 7-9
Through all the changing scenes of life,
In trouble and in joy,
The praises of my God shall still
My heart and tongue employ.
Of his deliv'rance I will boast,
Till all that are distressed
From my example comfort take,
And charm their griefs to rest.
O magnify the Lord with me,
With me exalt his Name;
When in distress to him I called,
He to my rescue came.
The hosts of God encamp around
The dwellings of the just;
Deliv'rance he affords to all
Who on his succor trust.
O make but trial of his love;
Experience will decide
How blest they are, and only they,
Who in his truth confide.
Fear him, ye saints; and you will then
lines.ko: 너희 성도들이여, 그를 두려워하라. 그리고 당신은 그때

lines.en: Have nothing else to fear:
Make you his service your delight,
lines.ko: 그분을 섬기는 일이 기쁨이 되도록 하십시오.

lines.en: He'll make your wants his care.
lines.ko: 그는 당신의 필요를 그의 관심으로 만들 것입니다.

title_en: Tell Me the Old, Old Story

lines.en: Tell me the old, old story
lines.ko: 주 예수 크신 사랑 늘 말해 주시오
lines.id: Pelita kaki kita, kemana kita menelusuri

lines.en: Of unseen things above,
lines.ko: 나 항상 듣던 말씀 또 들려 주시오
lines.id: Jalan kita ketika biasa tersesat

lines.en: Of Jesus and his glory,
lines.ko: 저 뵈지 않는 천국 주 예수 계신 곳
lines.id: Mengalir dari sumber rahmat surgawi,

lines.en: Of Jesus and his love:
lines.ko: 나 밝히 알아 듣게 또 들려 주시오
lines.id: Brook, di sisi musafir.

lines.en: Tell me the story simply,
lines.ko: 나 항상 듣던 말씀 나 항상 듣던 말씀
lines.id: Kata-kata yang selalu kudengar, kata-kata yang selalu kudengar

lines.en: As to a little child,
lines.ko: 주 예수 크신 사랑 또 들려주시오
lines.id: Tuhan Yesus, tolong beritahu saya sekali lagi kasih-Mu yang besar

lines.en: For I am weak and weary,
And helpless and defiled.
Tell me the old, old story,
lines.id: Roti jiwa kita, dimana kita memberi makan,

lines.en: Tell me the old, old story,
lines.ko: 내 기억 부족하여 늘 잊기 쉬우니
lines.id: Man-na sejati dari tempat tinggi

lines.en: Tell me the old, old story,
lines.ko: 잘 알아 듣기 쉽게 늘 말해 주시오
lines.id: Panduan dan bagan kami, tempat kami membaca

lines.en: Of Jesus and his love.
lines.ko: 날 구속 하신 사랑 또 들려 주시오
lines.id: Alam melampaui langit

lines.ko: 나 항상 듣던 말씀 나 항상 듣던 말씀
lines.id: Kata-kata yang selalu kudengar, kata-kata yang selalu kudengar

lines.ko: 주 예수 크신 사랑 또 들려주시오
lines.id: Tuhan Yesus, tolong beritahu saya sekali lagi kasih-Mu yang besar

lines.en: Tell me the story softly,
lines.ko: 주 예수 나를 위해 이 세상 오셔서
lines.id: Tiang api, menembus kegelapan,

lines.en: With earnest tones and grave;
lines.id: Atau awan yang sangat cerah di siang hari

lines.en: Remember, I'm the sinner
lines.ko: 나 같은 사람 위해 주 보혈 흘렸네
lines.id: Saat ombak menghantam kulit kita yang terombang-ambing

lines.en: Whom Jesus came to save:
lines.ko: 이 복스러운 말씀 또 들려 주시오
lines.id: Jangkar kami dan masa tinggal kami

lines.en: Tell me the story always,
lines.ko: 나 항상 듣던 말씀 나 항상 듣던 말씀
lines.id: Kata-kata yang selalu kudengar, kata-kata yang selalu kudengar

lines.en: If you would really be,
lines.ko: 주 예수 크신 사랑 또 들려주시오
lines.id: Tuhan Yesus, tolong beritahu saya sekali lagi kasih-Mu yang besar

lines.en: In any time of trouble,
A comforter to me.
Tell me the same old story,
lines.ko: 이 세상 헛된 영화 날 미혹할 때에
lines.id: Firman Tuhan yang selalu hidup,

lines.en: When you have cause to fear
lines.ko: 주 예수 크신 사랑 늘 들려 주시오
lines.id: Kehendak Putra-Nya yang mulia

lines.en: That this world's empty glory
lines.ko: 천국의 빛난 영광 내 눈에 비칠 때
lines.id: Tanpamu bagaimana bumi bisa diinjak,

lines.en: Is costing me too dear:
lines.ko: 주 예수 크신 사랑 또 들려 주시오
lines.id: Atau surga itu sendiri akan dimenangkan A-men.

lines.en: Yes, and when that world's glory
lines.ko: 나 항상 듣던 말씀 나 항상 듣던 말씀
lines.id: Kata-kata yang selalu kudengar, kata-kata yang selalu kudengar

lines.en: Is dawning on my soul,
lines.ko: 주 예수 크신 사랑 또 들려주시오
lines.id: Tuhan Yesus, tolong beritahu saya sekali lagi kasih-Mu yang besar

lines.en: Tell me the old, old story,
"Christ Jesus makes thee whole."

title_id: Ceritakan padaku Kisah Lama dan Lama

title_en: O Lord, by Grace Delivered

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: Behold the Throne of Grace!

lines.en: Behold the Throne of grace!
The promise calls me near:
There Jesus shows a smiling face
And waits to answer prayer.
My soul, ask what thou wilt;
Thou canst not be too bold;
Since his own blood for thee he spilt,
What else can he withhold?
lines.ko: 그는 또 무엇을 보류할 수 있습니까?

lines.en: Thine image, Lord, bestow,
Thy presence and thy love;
I ask to serve thee here below,
lines.ko: 나는 여기 아래에서 당신을 섬기기를 요청합니다.

lines.en: And reign with thee above.
Teach me to live by faith;
Conform my will to thine;
Let me victorious be in death,
And then in glory shine.

title_en: Come, My Soul, Thy Suit Prepare
title_ko: 오라 내 영혼아 네 옷을 준비하라 (자체번역)

lines.en: Come, my soul, thy suit prepare:
lines.ko: 오라, 내 영혼아, 네 옷을 준비하라:

lines.en: Jesus loves to answer pray'r;
He himself has bid thee pray,
Therefore will not say thee nay.
Thou art coming to a King,
Large petitions with thee bring;
For his grace and pow'r are such,
None can ever ask too much.
lines.ko: 누구도 너무 많은 것을 요구할 수 없습니다.

lines.en: With my burden I begin:
Lord, remove this load of sin;
lines.ko: 주님, 이 죄의 짐을 제거해 주소서.

lines.en: Let thy blood, for sinners spilt,
lines.ko: 죄인들을 위하여 주의 피를 흘리게 하소서

lines.en: Set my conscience free from guilt.
Lord, I come to thee for rest,
Take possession of my breast;
There thy blood-bought right maintain,
lines.ko: 거기에서 당신의 피로 사신 권리를 유지하십시오.

lines.en: And without a rival reign.
While I am a pilgrim here,
Let thy love my spirit cheer;
lines.ko: 당신의 사랑이 내 영혼을 응원하게 해주세요.

lines.en: As my Guide, my Guard, my Friend,
lines.ko: 나의 안내자, 나의 수호자, 나의 친구로서,

lines.en: Lead me to my journey's end.
lines.ko: 내 여행이 끝날 때까지 나를 이끌어주세요.

lines.en: Show me what I have to do,
Ev'ry hour my strength renew:
Let me live a life of faith,
Let me die thy people's death.
lines.ko: 당신 백성의 죽음으로 나도 죽게 하소서.

title_en: What a Friend We Have in Jesus

lines.en: What a Friend we have in Jesus,
lines.ko: 죄 짐 맡은 우리 구주 어찌 좋은 친군지
lines.id: Betapa besarnya Sahabat yang kita miliki di dalam Yesus,

lines.en: All our sins and griefs to bear!
lines.ko: 걱정 근심 무거운 짐 우리 주께 맡기세
lines.id: Semua dosa dan kesedihan kita harus ditanggung!

lines.en: What a privilege to carry
lines.ko: 주께 고함 없는 고로 복을 받지 못하네
lines.id: Sungguh sebuah hak istimewa untuk dibawa-bawa

lines.en: Ev'rything to God in prayer!
lines.id: Semuanya kepada Tuhan dalam doa!

lines.en: O what peace we often forfeit,
lines.id: Oh betapa kedamaian yang sering kita hilangkan,

lines.en: O what needless pain we bear,
lines.id: Oh, betapa tidak perlunya rasa sakit yang kita tanggung,

lines.en: All because we do not carry
lines.id: Semua karena kita tidak membawanya

lines.en: Ev'rything to God in prayer.
lines.id: Semuanya kepada Tuhan dalam doa.

lines.en: Have we trials and temptations?
lines.ko: 시험 걱정 모든 괴롬 없는 사람 누군가
lines.id: Sudahkah kita menghadapi cobaan dan godaan

lines.en: Is there trouble anywhere?
lines.ko: 부질없이 낙심 말고 기도 드려 아뢰세
lines.id: Apakah ada masalah dimana-mana

lines.en: We should never be discouraged:
lines.ko: 이런 진실 하신 친구 찾아 볼 수 있을까
lines.id: Kita tidak boleh berkecil hati:

lines.en: Take it to the Lord in prayer!
lines.ko: 우리 약함 아시오니 어찌 아니 아뢸까
lines.id: Bawalah itu kepada Tuhan dalam doa!

lines.en: Can we find a friend so faithful,
lines.id: Bisakah kita menemukan teman yang begitu setia,

lines.en: Who will all our sorrows share?
lines.id: Siapa yang akan berbagi semua kesedihan kita

lines.en: Jesus knows our ev'ry weakness
lines.id: Yesus mengetahui setiap kelemahan kita

lines.en: Take it to the Lord in prayer!
lines.id: Bawalah itu kepada Tuhan dalam doa!

lines.en: Are we weak and heavy laden,
lines.ko: 근심 걱정 무거운 짐 아니진 자 누군가
lines.id: Apakah kita lemah dan berat la-den,

lines.en: Cumbered with a load of care?
lines.ko: 피난처는 우리 예수 주께 기도 드리세
lines.id: Cum-bered dengan penuh perhatian

lines.en: Precious Saviour, still our Refuge
lines.ko: 세상 친구 멸시하고 너를 조롱 하여도
lines.id: Penyelamat yang Berharga, tetap Perlindungan kita

lines.en: Take it to the Lord in prayer!
lines.ko: 예수 품에 안기어서 참된 위로 받겠네 아멘
lines.id: Bawalah itu kepada Tuhan dalam doa!

lines.en: Do thy friends despise, forsake thee?
lines.id: Apakah teman-temanmu memandang rendah, demi kamu

lines.en: Take it to the Lord in prayer!
lines.id: Bawalah itu kepada Tuhan dalam doa!

lines.en: In his arms he'll take and shield thee,
lines.id: Dalam pelukan-Nya Dia akan mengambil dan melindungimu,

lines.en: Thou wilt find a solace there.
lines.id: Anda akan menemukan renda di sana, A-men.

title_id: Sungguh Sahabat yang Kita Miliki di dalam Yesus

title_en: Eternal Father, Strong to Save

lines.en: Eternal Father, strong to save,
Whose arm doth bind the restless wave,
lines.ko: 그 분의 팔이 불안한 파도를 묶으시고,

lines.en: Who bidd'st the mighty ocean deep
Its own appointed limits keep:
O hear us when we cry to thee
lines.ko: 오 우리가 당신께 부르짖을 때 우리의 말을 들어주소서

lines.en: For those in peril on the sea.
O Saviour, whose almighty word
The winds and waves submissive heard,
Who walkedst on the foaming deep
lines.ko: 누가 거품이 이는 깊은 곳을 걸었는가

lines.en: And calm amid its rage didst sleep:
lines.ko: 그리고 그 분노 속에서도 잠을 자지 못했습니다.

lines.en: O hear us when we cry to thee
lines.ko: 오 우리가 당신께 부르짖을 때 우리의 말을 들어주소서

lines.en: For those in peril on the sea.
O Sacred Spirit, who didst brood
Upon the chaos dark and rude,
Who badd'st its angry tumult cease,
lines.ko: 누가 그 분노한 소동을 멈추라고 했습니까?

lines.en: And gavest light and life and peace:
O hear us when we cry to thee
lines.ko: 오 우리가 당신께 부르짖을 때 우리의 말을 들어주소서

lines.en: For those in peril on the sea.
O Trinity of love and pow'r,
Our brethren shield in danger's hour;
lines.ko: 우리 형제들은 위험의 순간에 방패를 삼습니다.

lines.en: From rock and tempest, fire and foe,
Protect them wheresoe'er they go;
And ever let there rise to thee
Glad hymns of praise from land and sea.

title_en: From Every Stormy Wind That Blows

lines.en: From ev'ry stormy wind that blows,
lines.ko: 이 세상 풍파 심하고 또 환난 질고 많으나
lines.id: Dari setiap badai angin yang bertiup,

lines.en: From ev'ry swelling tide of woes,
lines.ko: 나 편히 쉬게 될 곳은 주 예비하신 주의 전
lines.id: Dari setiap gelombang kesengsaraan yang semakin besar,

lines.en: There is a calm, a sure retreat,
lines.id: Ada ketenangan, suguhan ulang yang pasti:

lines.en: 'Tis found beneath the mercy-seat.
lines.id: Ini ditemukan sebelum kursi belas kasihan.

lines.en: There is a place where Jesus sheds
lines.ko: 그 향기로운 기름을 주 내게 부어주셔서
lines.id: Ada tempat di mana Yesus ditumpahkan

lines.en: The oil of gladness on our heads,
lines.ko: 내 기쁨 더해주는 곳 주 피로 사신 주의 전
lines.id: Minyak kegembiraan di kepala kita

lines.en: A place than all besides more sweet;
lines.id: Tempat yang lebih manis dari semuanya:

lines.en: It is the blood-stained mercy-seat.
lines.id: Itu adalah kursi pertumpahan darah.

lines.en: There is a spot where spirits blend,
lines.ko: 주 믿는 형제자매들 그 몸은 떠나 있으나
lines.id: Ada adegan dimana roh menyatu,

lines.en: Where friend holds fellowship with friend,
lines.ko: 주 앞에 기도 드릴 곳 다 함께 모일 주의 전
lines.id: Dimana teman memegang kapal bersama temannya,

lines.en: Tho' sundered far; by faith they meet
lines.id: Meski jauh, karena iman mereka bertemu

lines.en: Around the common mercy-seat.
lines.id: Sebelum satu kursi belas kasihan bersama.

lines.en: Ah, whither could we flee for aid,
lines.ko: 내 손과 혀가 굳어도 내 몸의 피가 식어도
lines.id: Oh, biarkan tanganku melupakan keahliannya,

lines.en: When tempted, desolate, dismayed,
lines.ko: 나 영영 잊지 못할 곳 은혜의 보좌 주의 전 아멘
lines.id: Lidahku menjadi sunyi, dingin dan hening,

lines.en: Or how the hosts of hell defeat,
lines.id: Jantung yang terikat ini tak dapat dikalahkan,

lines.en: Had suff'ring saints no mercy-seat?
lines.id: Jika aku lupa mendapatkan kursi belas kasihan-Mu. Amin.

lines.en: There, there on eagle wings we soar,
And time and sense seem all no more,
And heav'n comes down our souls to greet,
And glory crowns the mercy-seat.
O may my hand forget her skill,
My tongue be silent, cold, and still,
This bounding heart forget to beat,
If I forget the mercy-seat.

title_id: Dari Setiap Angin Badai yang Berhembus

title_en: When the Weary, Seeking Rest

lines.en: When the weary, seeking rest,
To thy goodness flee;
When the heavy-laden cast
All their load on thee;
When the troubled, seeking peace,
On thy Name shall call;
When the sinner, seeking life,
At thy feet shall fall:
Hear then in love, O Lord, the cry
lines.ko: 주님, 사랑 안에서 부르짖는 소리를 들으소서

lines.en: In heav'n, thy dwelling place on high.
lines.ko: 천국에, 높은 곳에 당신의 거처가 있사옵니다.

lines.en: When the stranger asks a home,
All his toils to end;
When the hungry craveth food,
And the poor a friend;
When the widow weeps to thee,
Sad and lone and low;
When the orphan brings to thee
All his orphan woe:
When the worldling, sick at heart,
Lifts his soul above;
When the prodigal looks back
To his Father's love;
When the proud man, in his pride,
Stoops to seek thy face;
When the burdened brings his guilt
lines.ko: 무거운 짐을 진 자가 죄를 가져올 때

lines.en: To thy Throne of grace:

title_en: Father, We Thank You for the Night

lines.en: Father, we thank thee for the night,
And for the pleasant morning light;
lines.ko: 그리고 기분 좋은 아침 햇살을 위해;

lines.en: For rest and food and loving care,
And all that makes the day so fair.
lines.ko: 그리고 그 모든 것이 그날을 매우 공평하게 만듭니다.

lines.en: Help us to do the things we should,
lines.ko: 우리가 해야 할 일을 할 수 있도록 도와주세요.

lines.en: To be to others kind and good;
In all we do, in work, or play,
lines.ko: 우리가 하는 모든 일에서, 일할 때나 놀 때,

lines.en: To grow more loving ev'ry day.

alt_titles: Father, we thank thee for the night

title_en: Sweet Hour of Prayer

lines.en: Sweet hour of prayer, sweet hour of prayer,
lines.ko: 내 기도하는 그 시간 그 때가 가장 즐겁다
lines.id: Saat doa yang manis, saat doa yang manis,

lines.en: That calls me from a world of care,
lines.ko: 이 세상 근심 걱정에 얽매인 나를 부르사
lines.id: Itu memanggilku dari dunia perawatan,

lines.en: And bids me at my Father's throne,
lines.ko: 내 진정 소원 주 앞에 낱낱이 바로 아뢰어
lines.id: Dan mengajakku ke singgasana Ayahku,

lines.en: Make all my wants and wishes known!
lines.ko: 큰 불행 당해 슬플 때 나 위로 받게 하시네
lines.id: Sampaikan semua keinginan dan harapanku!

lines.en: In seasons of distress and grief,
lines.id: Di musim kesusahan dan kesedihan,

lines.en: My soul has often found relief,
lines.id: Jiwaku sering kali mendapat kelegaan,

lines.en: And oft escaped the tempter's snare,
lines.id: Dan sering kali luput dari godaan para penggoda,

lines.en: By thy return, sweet hour of prayer.
lines.id: Dengan kembalinyamu saat doa yang manis.

lines.en: Sweet hour of prayer, sweet hour of prayer,
lines.ko: 내 기도하는 그 시간 내게는 가장 귀하다
lines.id: Saat doa yang manis, saat doa yang manis,

lines.en: The joys I feel, the bliss I share
lines.ko: 저 광야 같은 세상을 끝없이 방황 하면서
lines.id: Kegembiraan yang saya rasakan, kebahagiaan yang saya bagikan

lines.en: Of those whose anxious spirits burn
lines.ko: 위태한 길로 나갈 때 주께서 나를 이끌어
lines.id: Dari mereka yang semangatnya membara

lines.en: With strong desires for thy return!
lines.ko: 그 보좌 앞에 나아가 큰 은혜 받게 하시네
lines.id: Dengan keinginan yang kuat untuk kedatangan-Mu kembali!

lines.en: With such I hasten to the place
lines.id: Dengan itu aku punya sepuluh sampai di tempat

lines.en: Where God, my Saviour, shows his face,
lines.id: Dimana Tuhan, Juruselamatku, menampakkan wajah-Nya.

lines.en: And gladly take my station there,
lines.id: Dan dengan senang hati mengambil tempatku di sana,

lines.en: And wait for thee, sweet hour of prayer.
lines.id: Dan tunggulah engkau, saat doa yang manis.

lines.en: Sweet hour of prayer, sweet hour of prayer,
lines.ko: 내 기도하는 그 시간 그 때가 가장 즐겁다
lines.id: Saat doa yang manis, saat doa yang manis,

lines.en: Thy wings shall my petition bear
lines.ko: 이때껏 지은 큰 죄로 내 마음 심히 아파도
lines.id: Sayap-sayapmu akan memikul permohonanku

lines.en: To him, whose truth and faithfulness
lines.ko: 참 마음으로 뉘우쳐 다 숨김없이 아뢰면
lines.id: Kepada Dia yang memiliki kebenaran dan kesetiaan

lines.en: Engage the waiting the soul to bless:
lines.ko: 주 나를 위해 복 주사 새 은혜 부어 주시네
lines.id: Libatkan jiwa yang menunggu untuk memberkati

lines.en: And since he bids me seek his face,
lines.id: Dan karena Dia memintaku mencari wajah-Nya,

lines.en: Believe his Word, and trust his grace,
lines.id: Percayalah pada Firman-Nya, dan percayalah pada kasih karunia-Nya,

lines.en: I'll cast on him my ev'ry care,
lines.id: Aku akan menyerahkan semua perhatianku pada-Nya,

lines.en: And wait for thee, sweet hour of prayer.
lines.id: Dan tunggulah engkau, saat doa yang manis.

lines.ko: 내 기도하는 그 시간 그 때가 가장 즐겁다
lines.id: Saat doa yang manis, saat doa yang manis,

lines.ko: 주 세상에서 일찍이 저 요란한 곳 피하여
lines.id: Tuhan kita sendiri mengetahui kegembiraan di sana

lines.ko: 빈 들에서나 산에서 온밤을 새워 지내사
lines.id: Dan sering kali, ketika berkerumun,

lines.ko: 주 예수 친히 기도로 큰 본을 보여 주셨네 아멘
lines.id: Akan menyelinap dan mencarinya,
Dan berdoa sepanjang malam di ladang atau bukit,
Sementara seluruh dunia kerang masih tumbuh.
Cara-Nya berjalan, Sukacita-Nya untuk dibagikan,
Inilah yang kami perlukan, saat berdoa yang manis. Amin

title_en: How Good It Is to Thank the Lord
title_ko: 주님께 감사하는 것이 얼마나 좋은 일입니까? (자체번역)

lines.en: From Psalm 92:1-9, 12-15
How good it is to thank the Lord,
And praise to thee, Most High, accord,
To show thy love with morning light,
lines.ko: 아침 햇살로 당신의 사랑을 보여주기 위해,

lines.en: And tell thy faithfulness each night;
lines.ko: 그리고 매일 밤 당신의 신실하심을 전하십시오.

lines.en: Yea, good it is thy praise to sing,
And all our sweetest music bring.
lines.ko: 그리고 우리의 가장 감미로운 음악은 모두 가져옵니다.

lines.en: O Lord, with joy my heart expands
lines.ko: 오 주님, 기쁨으로 내 마음은 넓어집니다

lines.en: Before the wonders of thy hands;
Great works, Jehovah, thou hast wrought,
Exceeding deep thine ev'ry thought;
A foolish man knows not their worth,
lines.ko: 어리석은 자는 자기의 가치를 알지 못하나니

lines.en: Nor he whose mind is of the earth.
lines.ko: 땅에 속한 마음을 가진 사람도 아닙니다.

lines.en: When as the grass the wicked grow,
When sinners flourish here below,
lines.ko: 죄인들이 이 땅 아래서 번성할 때,

lines.en: Then is there endless ruin nigh,
But thou, O Lord, art throned on high;
lines.ko: 그러나 주님, 당신은 높은 보좌에 앉으셨습니다.

lines.en: Thy foes shall fall before thy might,
lines.ko: 당신의 적들은 당신의 힘 앞에 쓰러질 것이며,

lines.en: The wicked shall be put to flight.
The righteous man shall flourish well,
And in the house of God shall dwell;
He shall be like a goodly tree,
And all his life shall fruitful be;
lines.ko: 그리고 그의 모든 삶은 열매를 맺을 것입니다.

lines.en: For righteous is the Lord and just,
He is my Rock, in him I trust.
lines.ko: 그분은 나의 반석이시니 나는 그분을 신뢰합니다.

title_en: Can a Little Child like Me
title_ko: 나 같은 어린 아이도 그럴 수 있을까? (자체번역)

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: What Shall I Render to My God

lines.en: From Psalm 116:12-19
What shall I render to my God
For all his kindness shown?
My feet shall visit thine abode,
lines.ko: 내 발이 당신의 거처를 방문할 것입니다.

lines.en: My songs address thy throne.
How much is mercy thy delight,
Thou ever blessed God!
How dear thy servants in thy sight!
lines.ko: 당신의 종들이 당신 눈에는 얼마나 사랑스럽습니까!

lines.en: How precious is their blood!
How happy all thy servants are!
How great thy grace to me!
My life, which thou hast made thy care,
Lord, I devote to thee.
Now I am thine, for ever thine,
lines.ko: 이제 나는 당신의 것입니다. 영원히 당신의 것입니다.

lines.en: Nor shall my purpose move,
Thy hand hath loosed my bonds of pain,
lines.ko: 당신의 손이 나의 고통을 풀어 주셨고,

lines.en: And bound me with thy love.
Here in thy courts I leave my vow,
lines.ko: 여기 당신의 궁정에서 나는 서원을 남깁니다.

lines.en: And thy rich grace record;
Witness, ye saints who hear me now,
lines.ko: 증인이여, 지금 내 말을 듣고 있는 성도 여러분,

lines.en: If I forsake the Lord.

title_en: Savior, Thy Dying Love Thou Gavest Me

lines.en: Sav-iour! Thy dy-ing love
lines.id: Penyelamat! Cintamu yang sekarat

lines.en: Thou gav-est me,
lines.id: Engkau memberiku,

lines.ko: 내 머리 숙여서 주님께 비는 말
lines.en: Nor should I aught wigh-hold,
lines.id: Aku juga tidak boleh menahan diri,

lines.en: Dear Lord, from Thee
lines.id: Ya Tuhan, dariMu

lines.en: In love my soul would bow,
lines.id: Dalam cinta, jiwaku akan tunduk,

lines.en: My heart ful-fill its vow
lines.id: Hatiku memenuhi sumpahnya

lines.en: Some of-fering bring Thee now,
lines.id: Beberapa persembahan membawakanMu sekarang,

lines.en: Some-thing for Thee.
lines.id: Sesuatu untukMu.

lines.en: At the blest mer-cy-seat,
lines.id: Di kursi belas kasihan yang terberkati,

lines.en: Plead-ing for me,
lines.id: Memohon padaku,

lines.en: My fee-ble raith looks up,
lines.id: Raithku yang lemah mendongak,

lines.en: Je-sus, to Thee,
lines.id: Yesus, bagiMu,

lines.en: Help me the cross to bear,
lines.id: Bantu aku memikul salib,

lines.en: Thy won-drous love de-clare,
lines.id: Cintamu yang menakjubkan terungkap,

lines.en: Some song to raise, or prayer,
lines.id: Beberapa lagu untuk diangkat, atau doa,

lines.en: Some-thing for Thee.
lines.id: Sesuatu untukMu.

lines.en: Give me a faith-ful heart,
lines.id: Beri aku hati yang setia,

lines.en: Guid-ed by Thee
lines.id: Dipandu oleh-Mu

lines.en: That each de-part-ing day
lines.id: Itu setiap hari keberangkatan

lines.en: Hence-forth may see
lines.id: Untuk seterusnya mungkin akan terlihat

lines.en: Some work of love be-gun,
lines.id: Beberapa pekerjaan cinta dimulai,

lines.en: Some deed of kind-ness done,
lines.id: Beberapa perbuatan baik telah dilakukan,

lines.en: Some wan-derer sought and won,
lines.id: Beberapa pengembara mencari dan menang,

lines.en: Some-thing for Thee.
lines.id: Sesuatu untukMu.

lines.en: All that I am and have-
lines.id: Semua yang saya miliki dan miliki-

lines.en: Thy gifts so free-
lines.id: Hadiahmu sangat gratis-

lines.en: In joy, in grief, thro' life,
lines.id: Dalam suka, duka, dalam hidup,

lines.en: Dear Lord, for Thee!
lines.id: Ya Tuhan, untukmu!

lines.en: And when Thy face I see,
lines.id: Dan ketika wajah-Mu kulihat,

lines.en: My ran-somed soul shall be
lines.id: Jiwaku yang terkoyak akan menjadi seperti itu

lines.en: Through all e-ter-ni-ty,
lines.id: Melalui segala kekekalan,

lines.en: Some-thing for Thee. A-men.
lines.id: Sesuatu untukMu. Amin.

title_en: Thy Life Was Given for Me

lines.en: Thy life was given for me,
Thy blood, O Lord, was shed,
That I might ransomed be,
And quickened from the dead;
Thy life was given for me;
What have I given for thee?
Long years were spent for me
In weariness and woe,
That through eternity
Thy glory I might know:
lines.ko: 나는 당신의 영광을 알 수 있습니다:

lines.en: Long years were spent for me;
Have I spent one for thee?
lines.ko: 내가 당신을 위해 한 푼이라도 썼나요?

lines.en: Thou, Lord, hast borne for me
More than my tongue can tell
lines.ko: 내 혀가 말할 수 있는 것보다 더 많은 걸

lines.en: Of bitt'rest agony,
To rescue me from hell;
Thou suff'redst all for me;
lines.ko: 당신은 나를 위해 모든 것을 참으셨습니다.

lines.en: What have I borne for thee?
And thou hast brought to me
Down from thy home above
Salvation full and free,
Thy pardon and thy love;
Great gifts thou broughtest me;
What have I brought to thee?
O let my life be given
My years for thee be spent;
lines.ko: 당신을 위한 나의 세월은 다 지나갔습니다.

lines.en: World-fetters all be riven
And joy with suff'ring blent:
Thou gav'st thyself for me,
lines.ko: 당신은 나를 위해 당신 자신을 바쳤습니다.

lines.en: I give myself to thee.

title_en: My Tribute

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: How Long Wilt Thou Forget Me

lines.en: From Psalm 13
How long wilt thou forget me,
O Lord, thou God of grace?
How long shall fears beset me,
While darkness hides thy face?
How long shall griefs distress me
And turn my day to night?
How long shall foes oppress me
And triumph in their might?
lines.ko: 그리고 그들의 힘으로 승리를 거둘 것인가?

lines.en: O Lord my God, behold me,
lines.ko: 오 여호와 나의 하나님이여 나를 보소서

lines.en: And hear my earnest cries;
Lest sleep of death enfold me,
Enlighten thou mine eyes;
Lest now my foe insulting
Should boast of his success,
And enemies exulting
Rejoice in my distress.
But I with expectation
Have on thy grace relied;
My heart in thy salvation
lines.ko: 내 마음은 주의 구원 안에 있습니다

lines.en: Shall still with joy confide;
And I with voice of singing
Will praise the Lord above,
Who, richest bounties bringing,
Hast dealt with me in love.

title_en: Be Thou My Vision

lines.en: Be Thou my Vi-sion, O Lord of my heart,
lines.id: Jadilah Engkau Visiku, ya Tuhan hatiku,

lines.en: Naught be all else to me, save that Thou art,
lines.id: Tidak ada yang lain bagiku, kecuali Engkau,

lines.en: Thou my best thought, by day or by night,
lines.id: Engkaulah pemikiran terbaikku, siang atau malam,

lines.ko: 잘 때나 깰 때 함께 하소서
lines.en: Wak-ing or sleep-ing, Thy pres-ence my light.
lines.id: Bangun atau tidur, kehadiran-Mu adalah cahayaku.

lines.en: Be Thou my Wis-dom, and Thou my true Word
lines.id: Jadilah Engkau Kebijaksanaanku, dan Engkaulah Firmanku yang sejati

lines.en: I ev-er with Thee and Thou with me, Lord
lines.id: Aku selalu bersamaMu dan Engkau bersamaku, Tuhan

lines.en: Thou my great Fa-ther, I Thy true son
lines.id: Engkau ayahku yang agung, aku putra sejatimu

lines.ko: 주 안에 내가 늘 함께 하네
lines.en: Thou in me dwell-ing, and I with Thee one.
lines.id: Engkau tinggal di dalamku, dan aku bersama Engkau.

lines.en: Rich-es I heed not, nor man's emp-ty praise,
lines.id: Orang kaya tidak kupedulikan, dan pujian kosong manusia pun tidak kupedulikan,

lines.en: Thou mine in-her-i-tance, now, and al-ways
lines.id: Engkau milikku, sekarang, dan selalu

lines.en: Thou and Thou on-ly, first in my heart,
lines.id: Hanya Engkau dan Engkau, yang pertama di hatiku,

lines.en: High king of heav-en, my Treas-ure Thou art.
lines.id: Raja tertinggi di surga, Engkau adalah Harta Karunku.

lines.en: High King of heav-en, my vic-to-ry won,
lines.id: Raja Agung surga, kemenanganku dimenangkan,

lines.en: May I reach heav-en's joys, O bright heaven's Sun!
lines.id: Semoga aku mencapai kebahagiaan surga, wahai Matahari surga yang cerah!

lines.en: Heart of my own heart, what-ev-er be-fall,
lines.id: Hati hatiku sendiri, apa pun yang terjadi,

lines.en: Still be my Vi-sion, O Rul-er of all. A-men.
lines.id: Tetaplah menjadi Visiku, wahai Penguasa segalanya. Amin.

title_en: Judge Me, God of My Salvation

lines.en: From Psalm 43
Judge me, God of my salvation,
Plead my cause, for thee I trust:
lines.ko: 내가 당신을 신뢰하므로 내 주장을 변호해 주십시오.

lines.en: Hear my earnest supplication,
Save me from my foes unjust.
O my soul, why art thou grieving?
What disquiets and dismays?
Hope in God, his help receiving,
lines.ko: 하나님께 소망을 두어 그의 도움을 받으시며

lines.en: I shall yet my Saviour praise.
lines.ko: 나는 여전히 나의 구세주를 찬양할 것입니다.

lines.en: For my Strength, my God, thou art:
Why am I cast off by thee
In the sorrow of my heart,
While the foe oppresses me?
Light and truth, my way attending,
Send thou forth to be my guide,
Till thy holy mount ascending,
I within thy house abide.
At thy sacred altar bending,
God, my God, my boundless joy,
lines.ko: 하느님, 나의 하느님, 나의 무한한 기쁨이시여,

lines.en: Harp and voice, in worship blending,
For thy praise will I employ.
O my soul, why art thou grieving?
What disquiets and dismays?
Hope in God; his help receiving,
lines.ko: 하나님께 소망을 두라. 그의 도움을 받고,

lines.en: I shall yet my Saviour praise.
lines.ko: 나는 여전히 나의 구세주를 찬양할 것입니다.

title_en: May the Mind of Christ My Savior
title_ko: 그리스도의 마음이 나의 구세주가 되기를 바랍니다 (자체번역)

lines.en: [English lyrics not available in the database for this revised hymn]
lines.ko: [이 개정된 찬송가의 데이터베이스에는 영어 가사가 없습니다.]

title_en: Jesus, the Very Thought of Thee

lines.en: Jesus, the very thought of thee
lines.id: Ya Tuhan, pikiranmu yang sebenarnya

lines.en: With sweetness fills my breast;
lines.ko: 주 얼굴 뵈올 때에야 얼마나 좋으랴
lines.id: Dengan manisnya memenuhi dadaku

lines.en: But sweeter far thy face to see,
lines.id: Namun jauh lebih manis melihat wajah-Mu,

lines.en: And in thy presence rest.
lines.id: Dan di hadirat-Mu istirahatlah.

lines.en: Nor voice can sing, nor heart can frame,
lines.id: Suara juga tidak dapat menyanyi, dan hati tidak dapat membingkai,

lines.en: Nor can the mem'ry find,
lines.ko: 천지에 있는 이름 중 비할 데 없도다
lines.id: Mem-'ry juga tidak dapat menemukannya

lines.en: A sweeter sound than thy blest Name,
lines.id: Suara yang lebih merdu dari nama-Mu yang terberkati,

lines.en: O Saviour of mankind.
lines.id: O Sav-iour of mankind!

lines.en: O Hope of ev'ry contrite heart,
lines.ko: 참 회개 하는 자에게 소망이 되시고
lines.id: Wahai Harapan dari setiap hati yang menyesal,

lines.en: O Joy of all the meek,
lines.id: Wahai Kegembiraan semua orang yang lemah lembut,

lines.en: To those who fall, how kind thou art!
lines.id: Kepada mereka yang bertanya, betapa baiknya Engkau!

lines.en: How good to those who seek!
lines.id: Betapa baiknya bagi mereka yang mencari!

lines.en: But what to those who find? Ah, this
lines.ko: 예수의 넓은 사랑을 어찌 다 말하랴
lines.id: Namun bagaimana dengan mereka yang menemukannya? Ah! ini

lines.en: Nor tongue nor pen can show:
lines.ko: 주 사랑 받은 사람만 그 사랑 알도다
lines.id: Baik lidah maupun pena tidak dapat terlihat.

lines.en: The love of Jesus, what it is
lines.id: Kasih Yesus, apa adanya,

lines.en: None but his loved ones know.
lines.id: Tidak seorang pun kecuali orang-orang yang dikasihi-Nya yang mengetahuinya.

lines.en: Jesus, our only Joy be thou,
lines.ko: 사랑의 구주 예수여 내 기쁨 되시고
lines.id: Ya Tuhan! satu-satunya kegembiraan kami adalah Engkau,

lines.en: As thou our Prize wilt be;
lines.id: Sesuai dengan Engkau, hadiah kami akan menjadi

lines.en: Jesus, be thou our Glory now,
lines.id: Ya Tuhan! jadilah Engkau kemuliaan kami sekarang,

lines.en: And through eternity.
lines.id: Dan melalui e-ter-ni-ty. Amin.

title_en: Jesus, Thou Joy of Loving Hearts

lines.en: Jesus, thou Joy of loving hearts,
Thou Fount of life, thou Light of men,
From the best bliss that earth imparts
We turn unfilled to thee again.
lines.ko: 우리는 채워지지 않은 채 다시 당신께로 돌아갑니다.

lines.en: Thy truth unchanged hath ever stood;
Thou savest those that on thee call;
To them that seek thee thou art good,
To them that find thee all in all.
We taste thee, O thou living Bread,
lines.ko: 우리는 당신을 맛봅니다, 오 당신의 살아 있는 빵이여,

lines.en: And long to feast upon thee still;
lines.ko: 그리고 여전히 당신을 향하여 잔치를 벌이기를 갈망합니다.

lines.en: We drink of thee, the Fountainhead,
And thirst our souls from thee to fill.
lines.ko: 그리고 우리 영혼을 당신에게서 채우려고 목말라 합니다.

lines.en: Our restless spirits yearn for thee,
Where'er our changeful lot is cast;
Glad when thy gracious smile we see,
Blest when our faith can hold thee fast.
lines.ko: 우리의 믿음이 당신을 굳게 붙들 수 있을 때 축복을 받습니다.

lines.en: O Jesus, ever with us stay,
lines.ko: 오 예수여, 언제나 우리와 함께 계십시오.

lines.en: Make all our moments calm and bright;
lines.ko: 우리의 모든 순간을 차분하고 밝게 만드십시오.

lines.en: Chase the dark night of sin away,
Shed o'er the world thy holy light.

alt_titles: Jesus, thou Joy of loving hearts (tune 2)

title_en: How Sweet the Name of Jesus Sounds

lines.en: How sweet the Name of Jesus sounds
In a believer's ear!
It soothes his sorrows, heals his wounds,
lines.ko: 그의 슬픔을 달래주고 그의 상처를 치유하며

lines.en: And drives away his fear.
It makes the wounded spirit whole,
And calms the troubled breast;
'Tis manna to the hungry soul,
And to the weary rest.
Dear Name! the Rock on which I build,
lines.ko: 친애하는 이름! 내가 그 위에 건축하는 반석,

lines.en: My Shield and hiding place,
My never-failing Treasury filled
lines.ko: 한 번도 실패하지 않는 나의 금고가 가득 찼어요

lines.en: With boundless stores of grace;
Jesus, my Shepherd, Brother, Friend,
My Prophet, Priest, and King,
My Lord, my Life, my Way, my End,
lines.ko: 나의 주님, 나의 생명, 나의 길, 나의 끝,

lines.en: Accept the praise I bring.
Weak is the effort of my heart,
And cold my warmest thought;
lines.ko: 그리고 나의 가장 따뜻한 생각은 차갑습니다.

lines.en: But when I see thee as thou art,
lines.ko: 그러나 내가 당신의 모습을 볼 때,

lines.en: I'll praise thee as I ought.
Till then I would thy love proclaim
With ev'ry fleeting breath;
And may the music of thy Name
Refresh my soul in death.
lines.ko: 죽음 속에서 내 영혼을 새롭게 하소서.

title_en: My Jesus, I Love Thee
title_ko: 내 주 되신 주를 참 사랑하고

lines.en: My Jesus, I love thee, I know thou art mine;
lines.ko: 내 주 되신 주를 참 사랑하고
lines.id: Yesusku, aku cinta padaMu, aku tahu Engkau milikku

lines.en: For thee all the follies of sin I resign.
lines.id: BagiMu segala kebodohan dosa kutandatangani kembali.

lines.en: My gracious Redeemer, my Saviour art thou;
lines.id: Pertimbanganku yang Maha Pemurah, Penyelamatku adalah Engkau

lines.en: If ever I loved thee, my Jesus, 'tis now.
lines.id: Jika dulu aku mencintaiMu, Yesusku, sekaranglah saatnya.

lines.en: I love thee because thou hast first loved me,
lines.id: Aku mencintai-Mu karena Engkau yang lebih dulu mencintaiku,

lines.en: And purchased my pardon on Calvary's tree.
lines.id: Dan membeli pengampunanku di pohon Cal-va-ry.

lines.en: I love thee for wearing the thorns on thy brow;
lines.ko: 그 가시관 쓰신 내 주 뵈오니
lines.id: Aku mencintai-Mu karena memakai duri di alis-Mu

lines.en: If ever I loved thee, my Jesus, 'tis now.
lines.id: Jika dulu aku mencintaiMu, Yesusku, sekaranglah saatnya.

lines.en: I'll love thee in life, I will love thee in death;
lines.ko: 내 평생에 힘 쓸 그 큰 의무는
lines.id: Aku akan mencintai-Mu dalam hidup, aku akan mencintai-Mu dalam kematian

lines.en: And praise thee as long as thou lendest me breath;
lines.id: Dan pujilah Engkau selama Engkau memberiku nafas

lines.en: And say, when the death-dew lies cold on my brow:
lines.ko: 숨질 때에 까지 내 할 말씀은
lines.id: Dan katakanlah, ketika embun kematian terasa dingin di keningku

lines.en: If ever I loved thee, my Jesus, 'tis now.
lines.id: Jika dulu aku mencintaiMu, Yesusku, sekaranglah saatnya.

lines.en: In mansions of glory and endless delight,
lines.id: Di rumah-rumah mewah dan kesenangan tiada akhir,

lines.en: I'll ever adore thee in heaven so bright;
lines.id: Aku akan selalu memuja-Mu di surga yang begitu terang

lines.en: I'll sing with the glittering crown on my brow:
lines.ko: 금 면류관 쓰고 나 찬송할 말

lines.en: If ever I loved thee, my Jesus, 'tis now.

title_en: More Love to Thee, O Christ

lines.en: More love to thee, O Christ,
lines.id: Lebih banyak cinta kepada-Mu, ya Kristus, Lebih banyak cinta kepada-Mu!

lines.en: More love to thee!
lines.id: Dengarlah doa yang kupanjatkan dengan lutut tertekuk

lines.id: Inilah permohonanku yang sungguh-sungguh, Lebih banyak cinta, ya Kristus, kepada-Mu,

lines.en: On bended knee;
lines.id: Lebih banyak cinta padamu, lebih banyak cinta padamu!

lines.en: This is my earnest plea,
More love, O Christ, to thee,
More love to thee,
More love to thee.
Once earthly joy I craved,

lines.en: Sought peace and rest;
lines.id: Kini hanya Engkau yang kucari, Berikan yang terbaik

lines.en: Now thee alone I seek;
lines.ko: 다만 내 비는 말 내 구주 예수를
lines.id: Ini semua doaku agar lebih banyak cinta, ya Kristus, kepada-Mu,

lines.en: Give what is best:
lines.id: Lebih banyak cinta padamu, lebih banyak cinta padamu!

lines.en: This all my prayer shall be,
More love, O Christ, to thee,
More love to thee,
More love to thee.
Let sorrow do its work,
lines.id: Maka nafas terakhirku akan membisikkan puji-pujian kepada-Mu

lines.en: Send grief and pain;
lines.id: Ini adalah seruan perpisahan yang akan membuat hatiku terangkat

lines.en: Sweet are thy messengers,
lines.ko: 다만 내 비는 말 내 구주 예수를
lines.id: Ini tetap doanya agar lebih banyak cinta, ya Kristus, kepada-Mu,

lines.en: Sweet their refrain,
lines.id: Lebih banyak cinta padamu, lebih banyak cinta padamu!

lines.en: When they can sing with me,
More love, O Christ, to thee,
More love to thee,
More love to thee.
Then shall my latest breath
Whisper thy praise;
This be the parting cry
My heart shall raise,
This still its prayer shall be,
More love, O Christ, to thee,
More love to thee,
More love to thee.

title_en: He Lifted Me

lines.en: In lovingkindness Jesus came
My soul in mercy to reclaim,
And from the depths of sin and shame
Through grace he lifted me.
lines.ko: 은혜를 통해 그분은 나를 들어 올리셨습니다.

lines.en: From sinking sand he lifted me,

lines.en: With tender hand he lifted me,
lines.ko: 그는 부드러운 손으로 나를 들어 올렸고,

lines.en: From shades of night to plains of light,
Oh, praise his name, he lifted me!
lines.ko: 오, 그의 이름을 찬양하라, 그가 나를 일으켜 세웠도다!

lines.en: He called me long before I heard,

lines.en: Before my sinful heart was stirred,
But when I took him at his word,
lines.ko: 그러나 내가 그의 말을 그대로 받아들였을 때,

lines.en: Forgiv'n he lifted me.
His brow was pierced with many a thorn,
His hands by cruel nails were torn,
When from my guilt and grief, forlorn,
lines.ko: 나의 죄책감과 슬픔으로 인해 외로워질 때,

lines.ko: 사랑에 빠진 그는 나를 들어 올렸습니다.

lines.ko: 이제 나는 더 높은 차원에 거주하며,

lines.en: Yet how or why, I cannot tell,

lines.en: He should have lifted me.

alt_titles: In loving-kindness, Jesus came
He lifted Me

title_en: Savior, Teach Me, Day by Day

lines.en: Saviour, teach me, day by day,
Love's sweet lesson to obey;
Sweeter lesson cannot be,
lines.ko: 이보다 더 달콤한 교훈은 있을 수 없습니다.

With a child's glad heart of love,
At thy bidding may I move;
lines.ko: 당신의 명령에 따라 제가 움직일 수 있게 하소서.

lines.en: Prompt to serve and follow thee,

Teach me thus thy steps to trace,
lines.ko: 따라가는 당신의 발걸음을 나에게 가르쳐 주십시오.

lines.en: Strong to follow in thy grace;

Loving him who first loved me.
Love in loving finds employ,
In obedience all her joy;
Ever new that joy will be,
lines.ko: 그 기쁨은 언제나 새로운 것이 될 것입니다.

lines.en: Loving him who first loved me.

title_en: O Safe to the Rock That Is Higher Than I

lines.en: O safe to the Rock that is higher than I
My soul in its conflicts and sorrows would fly;
lines.ko: 내 영혼은 갈등과 슬픔 속에서 날아갈 것입니다.

lines.en: So sinful, so weary, thine, thine would I be;
lines.ko: 나는 너무나 죄 많고 지쳤으며, 당신의 것, 당신의 것입니다.

lines.en: Thou blest Rock of Ages, I'm hiding in thee.
lines.ko: 당신께서 만세의 반석을 축복하셨으니, 나는 당신 안에 숨어 있습니다.

lines.en: Hiding in thee, hiding in thee
lines.ko: 네 안에 숨어, 네 안에 숨어

lines.en: Thou blest Rock of Ages, I'm hiding in thee.
lines.ko: 당신께서 만세의 반석을 축복하셨으니, 나는 당신 안에 숨어 있습니다.

lines.en: In the calm of the noontide, in sorrow's lone hour,
lines.ko: 정오의 고요함 속에서, 슬픔의 외로운 시간 속에서,
